steps.title: Identify the component shapes
steps.content: The figure combines a rectangle and a semicircle. The rectangle has width $WX = 20$ and height $WZ = 5$. The semicircle sits on top with diameter 20.

steps.title: Calculate the semicircle area and sum
steps.content:
The semicircle has diameter 20, so radius = 10.
$$\frac{1}{2}\pi(10)^2 = 50\pi$$
Total area = rectangle + semicircle:
$$20 \times 5 + 50\pi$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — '20+5+10π': Adds the dimensions instead of multiplying to get rectangle area, and uses 10π which is neither the full circle nor correct semicircle area. ✗
  • Choice B — '20 × 5 + 10π': Rectangle area 100 is correct, but 10π = ½π(10)2 / 5 does not match the semicircle formula; the semicircle area should be ½πr2 = 50π. ✗
  • Choice C — '20 × 5 + 50π': Rectangle area = 20 × 5 = 100; semicircle radius = 10, area = ½π(10)2 = 50π. Total = 20 × 5 + 50π. ✓
  • Choice D — '20 × 5 + 100π': 100π is the area of a full circle with radius 10, not a semicircle. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
